essence: Signal Studio (built off an older AppyStack) needs a gap analysis to absorb new AppyStack capabilities without breaking the app; Mochaccino should prototype alternate UX on cloned/reshaped data because UX changes must not casually alter data shape — the core of the Angela (UX-first) vs David/Ronnie (data-first) tension.

essence: Full audit of GitHub orgs/repos/domains: appydave.com is dead and will be rebuilt as the main public brand + content hub (resources, content pillars, gated material, docs), legacy clutter (Klueless sites, appydave-flivideo) gets archived, appydave-awb may rename to agentworkflowbuilder.com, brains move down one folder level, and BMAD learnings need a decided home with an absolute path.

essence: SupportSignal BMAD v6 kickoff housekeeping: BMAD learnings go to a new public-ish GitHub repo (with a two-way Brian liaison doc where their agents talk to each other, Jan getting edit access for graphics), the app domain is settled as app.supportsignal.com.au, and a cleanup pass plus Playwright-driven UX/accessibility review of the MVP is authorized.

essence: Strategy call with Rodi: build order is schema+auth first then incidents/moments for field use (back-office management screens later), the MVP is a reference implementation AI rebuilds the real app from (JSON→Supabase), Angela is over-refining MVP UI because she conflates visuals with data — so tomorrow's three-way session sets shared guardrails on flow, priorities and who decides what.

actions.text: Hold the guardrails alignment session with Angela and Rodi to agree the high-level flow, data-vs-visualization distinction, and change-introduction rules

essence: Idea for a /loop command distinct from cron jobs — short in-session loops (20 min in 5-min increments, e.g. push-then-watch-CI) and standing ingestion loops in the brain that continuously process incoming OMI/email/chat data, route it, file it and summarize it; framed alongside a personal note about brain fog and diet struggles in Thailand.

actions.text: Explore a /loop-driven background ingestion pipeline that routes incoming OMI, email and chat data to the right brain locations

essence: After new code landed on workspace-automation (DeckHand), David asked to test each Ecamm button individually to confirm it executes correctly.

actions.text: Deep background research: can the Stream Deck SDK (or reverse engineering) read decks/profiles/buttons/config, and what workarounds exist for the platform limits

essence: UAT plan verified to cover incidents/dashboard with a run-everything pass planned (fix or write recommendations, delete useless context-poisoning tests), plus Stream Deck UI corrections: bigger right-panel fonts/width, proper device→profile→actions model instead of three separate decks, and missing basics like button drag and color/name editing.

actions.text: Run every UAT and either fix issues or compile a recommendation document; delete tests that don't reflect reality
Fix Stream Deck UI: larger fonts/wider right section, device→profile→actions hierarchy, draggable buttons, editable colors and names — follow Elgato's established best practices

essence: Sharp critique of DeckHand's prompt-template UI: the unrequested prompt-template section is still on the main screen despite an earlier removal request, the copy button is missing on the template page, and the right side must show actual schema values/structures — curly-brace placeholders and format names without schemas are useless.
